pages.body:
# Computer Use

Computer Use is the supervised local desktop-control broker. It shows the live display stream, permission state, pending approval prompts, and recent computer-use activity for agents that are allowed to request screen or input actions.

## Use it for

- Bless capable agents, arm or disarm control, and supervise the live stage.
- Approve or reject pending screen/input actions.
- Use pause, disarm, or panic controls quickly when local control should stop.

## Current guardrails

Who can drive reads the HR Manager all-team roster, but every grant remains scoped to that agent's team. The attached MCP config receives a team/name authority, the broker token map stores that scoped authority, and the arm-time bless list checks the same value before allowing screenshots or input. Old bare-name tokens are blocked by scoped arming and appear as a Legacy authority review notice near Who can drive, so operators can see stale access artifacts without automatically reauthorizing them. Review helpers can copy the scoped target and add the current agent to the batch Bless selection, leaving the old token blocked.

Arm, Bless, Remove, safety-mode changes, and approval decisions now fresh-read broker or roster state before continuing. Arm previews the all-team attached-agent list before and after confirmation, then asks the main process to derive the actual arm list from the current attached agents in each synced team. The main process compares the reviewed attached-agent stamp, preserves other teams' armed authorities, and refuses stale renderer-provided blessed lists. Background armed-list refreshes after Bless or Remove send the same attached-agent stamp, so a stale manager read cannot silently rewrite the broker's live blessed set. Empty Arm for live-view permission testing now requires checking Start live view with no blessed agents before the Arm button enables. Who can drive uses a multi-select HR-synced roster that prunes stale selections on team/roster changes and disables batch submission unless at least one selected agent is still eligible and unblessed. Batch Bless fresh-reads every selected agent row and MCP metadata before and after confirmation, blocks moved or changed targets, and the bridge only attaches rows whose current roster ids still match the reviewed agents. Remove re-reads the attached row before and after confirmation, detaches only the current scoped authority, the bridge refuses same-name replacement rows, rebuilds only when that exact agent id still exists in the current roster, and refreshes the armed list if needed; stale authority for a moved or deleted agent is removed without rebuilding a same-named replacement. Allow/Deny re-reads the pending request and blocks stale or expired approval prompts. Approval prompts are marked locally resolved only after the broker accepts the decision; if confirmation fails while the request is still pending, the page refreshes pending approvals and leaves it visible for review. Disabling supervised per-action approval fresh-reads broker mode and still requires confirmation.

Screen Recording and Accessibility remain strict hard gates for capture and input. Input Monitoring and Automation are best-effort macOS TCC readbacks: if macOS blocks direct inspection or Automation has not recorded a per-app grant yet, the permissions card renders an amber Needs verification state with System Settings guidance instead of a red denial. Confirmed denied, restricted, or not-determined states still render as missing permissions.

Pause, Disarm, and PANIC remain immediate stop paths and are not blocked by long attach/rebuild work. PANIC still disarms, clears pending approvals, releases held input, and records the stop in the broker audit.

## Safety boundary

Actions should run only through the armed broker and approval flow. The page depends on local OS permissions such as Screen Recording and Accessibility. Navigating away stops the live renderer view, although global notifications can still report relevant broker state. Conservative default: Arm with no blessed agents is still available for live-view or permission testing only after the empty-session review is armed; a stricter operator policy could require at least one blessed agent before arming.
implementedTwoHundredThirtyFirstPass: Computer Use Who can drive now selects eligible agents from the HR Manager all-team roster instead of only the active team.
Bless selections, attached rows, legacy authority helpers, and already-blessed checks now use team-qualified keys so same-name or same-id agents across teams cannot collide.
Arm now reviews and captures the merged all-team attached list, while attach, detach, rebuild, and armed-list refreshes still execute against each agent's own team-scoped authority.
Validation: npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki drift, diff check, and npm run build passed before release validation.

pages.purpose: Infrastructure panel for host hardware, manager connection, coordinator status with focused HR Manage handoff, self-update, subscriptions, local models, image server, local stacks, and inference backends.
pages.scope: Local app settings plus manager-facing provider/concurrency routes.

tiles.label: Manager/local/backend diagnostics
tiles.description: Manager compatibility, provider routing, local runtime readiness, synced backends, and quick fixes are shown inside the cards that own those systems instead of a separate first-run checkpoint.

tiles.label: Hardware
tiles.description: Read-only host compute summary.

tiles.label: Connection
tiles.description: Manager URL, active team, read-only coordinator status, and focused HR Manage handoff for routing changes.

tiles.label: Self-update
tiles.description: Version, staged/latest status, auto-download toggle, check now, main-process-owned background checks, explicit Restart & update apply, and gated stale/consumed staged zip pruning.

tiles.label: Managed subscription sign-ins
tiles.description: CLI/OAuth sign-in, install, and status rows for managed runtime options only: Claude, ChatGPT/Codex, Cursor, Grok, Antigravity, GitHub Copilot, Kiro, and legacy q when installed. Rows distinguish live CLI-confirmed signed-in state from account-linked cache evidence, auto-check account status and runtime model/freshness routes on Settings focus with a one-minute guard plus every 5 minutes while mounted, detect installed binaries/apps after visible installer handoff, show safe account labels from provider status/cache metadata, keep account launches inside Settings, render Sign out only when a reviewed logout command exists for an installed/linked account, and keep API-key providers on the Inference backends path.

tiles.label: Local models & backends
tiles.description: Compact local model/backend status for Ollama installs, routing readiness, stack installs, and catalog state; guarded next-step setup actions only when needed; quiet concurrency; passive installed-model chips; actionable update rows only when catalog digests change; downloadable model catalog; and public Ollama library checks that surface new tags without automatically downloading models. The setup state distinguishes installed/configured from synced/route-ready before agents rely on local runtimes.

tiles.label: Local image generator
tiles.description: Configures an optional local-first image server URL and API style for chat image generation, with image-capable API backends as fallback. The URL input is an unsaved draft until Save succeeds, saved preferences are probed separately from generation availability, unreachable saved servers render as fallback-needed, and Scan local checks Automatic1111/Forge plus configured loopback OpenAI-compatible providers before common image API defaults.

tiles.label: Local LLM stacks
tiles.description: Local inference stack catalog. It opens on a compact backend-presets filter, keeps common filters visible, moves advanced stack tags behind a tag dropdown, reviews install commands before visible Terminal runs, shows unavailable one-click installs with concrete reason labels such as Linux host required, choose model first, or manual setup, shows uninstall only after matching package/container install evidence, derives claimed ports from default/API/command host mappings, suppresses self-conflict warnings for a stack's own configured/discovered backend port, warns when another claimed port is running or configured, can auto-rewrite Docker -p, --port, and --tcp install commands to a free loopback port, separates package install from backend add, includes shared-port backend presets such as LocalAI, MLX, and TGI, runs MLX through an app-managed background start/stop path on port 8081 with local logs, auto-adds pending Inference backend rows for installed local provider stacks, auto-upgrades matching live backend presets after Scan running, keeps row-level Add backend as a fresh-scan fallback, and keeps ambient focus/interval discovery throttled and read-only so backend state is not mutated by window focus alone.

tiles.label: Inference backends
tiles.description: API/cloud-focused provider catalog, custom API endpoint add, NVIDIA API Catalog preset, Perplexity API key support through PERPLEXITY_API_KEY/PPLX_API_KEY or pasted key, add/remove/default/toggle/connect/model sync, provider-specific key readiness, pending installed local stack rows, successful local Connect & sync promotion to enabled, live no-model probes rendered as warning/not route-ready, quiet already-configured no-op state, and guarded same-name replacement when the endpoint or hidden key actually changes. Local server creation is owned by Local LLM stacks.

fields.label: Parallel inference cap
fields.description: Desired manager local-model concurrency, 1-16.

fields.label: Download model
fields.description: Ollama model id to pull.

fields.label: search models
fields.description: Filters local model catalog.

fields.label: Image URL
fields.description: Optional local image server endpoint; unset means use an image-capable API backend when available.

fields.label: Provider name
fields.description: Name for a custom inference backend.

fields.label: Base URL
fields.description: OpenAI-compatible or provider endpoint.

fields.label: API key
fields.description: Provider key stored in local settings or resolved from env; cloud/key-required presets show the helper hint, Perplexity also resolves PERPLEXITY_API_KEY/PPLX_API_KEY, while local OpenAI-compatible servers stay keyless and quiet.

dropdowns.label: Local image API style
dropdowns.description: Stable Diffusion WebUI or OpenAI Images API.

dropdowns.label: Provider catalog
dropdowns.description: API/cloud provider picker that prefills kind/base URL, provider key requirement, notes, and preset model chips for common backends such as NVIDIA API Catalog. Local presets are handled by Local LLM stacks.

dropdowns.label: Provider kind
dropdowns.description: Classifies custom API backend behavior and model compatibility; local provider kinds are added through Local LLM stacks scan evidence.

controls.label: Check now
controls.description: Checks release manifest for updates, stages newer builds, and gates stale staged download pruning before reporting update status.

controls.label: Auto-download
controls.description: Persists whether a found update should download and stage automatically; applying still requires the explicit Restart & update action.

controls.label: Install / open / sign out
controls.description: Runs reviewed CLI installers visibly, auto-rechecks installed-state evidence, launches managed account flows from IDACC for installed providers, and renders sign-out only when installed evidence exists and a safe provider command is available. The card quietly refreshes account status and warms model/freshness routes on open, focus, and every 5 minutes; API-key providers such as Perplexity and Gemini API are handled by the Inference backends provider dropdown.

controls.label: Download/Remove model
controls.description: Pulls or removes Ollama models with progress; re-pull/update actions are also surfaced from the Ollama catalog digest check when an installed tag differs from the public library digest.

controls.label: Check catalog
controls.description: Fetches public Ollama library tag pages for watched local-model families, compares remote tag digests with installed `/api/tags` digests, and lists newly discovered tags from outside the curated catalog. The check is read-only and never pulls models without a user action.

controls.label: Scan running
controls.description: Scans local inference servers from Local LLM stacks and auto-adds or upgrades matching live backend presets; installed-but-not-running local provider stacks are reconciled into disabled pending Inference backend rows, shared-port 8080 servers such as LocalAI, MLX, and TGI remain explicit choices, and reviewed alternate install ports are probed as loopback-only extra candidates.

controls.label: Install / Uninstall stack
controls.description: Shows command-backed start-here/easy/guided/advanced stacks as Install actions, reviews runnable stack install/uninstall commands, detects default/API/command host-port conflicts before Terminal opens, keeps Terminal visible with clipboard fallback, polls install evidence after the handoff, and keeps package install separate from backend add/removal.

controls.label: Readiness quick actions
controls.description: Re-check manager extension compatibility, copy the manager compatibility report, download starter, add Ollama backend, sync a stale backend, or scan running local servers from the top checkpoint and Local models path strip. Default pinning stays optional in the provider table.

controls.label: Open HR Manage
controls.description: Hands coordinator and routing changes to HR Manager Manage > Hierarchy instead of mutating coordinator state from Settings.

controls.label: Default star
controls.description: Sets provider default.

controls.label: Enabled checkbox
controls.description: Enables/disables provider.

controls.label: Connect & sync
controls.description: Validates provider and syncs model list.

displays.label: Readiness summary
displays.description: Shows manager online/offline, Control Center manager-extension /capabilities feature/route readiness, open-or-pinned routing state, synced local backend readiness, ready backend count, synced count, and missing key count. The action row is quiet when ready and only shows contextual fixes for unsynced/no backends or manager compatibility issues.

displays.label: Local setup status
displays.description: Compact Local models strip that shows the next setup state: download starter, add Ollama backend, sync local backend, or backend ready. Download/Add/Sync buttons stay in the strip; Scan running lives once in the card header.

displays.label: Hardware summary
displays.description: Chip, cores, GPU, memory, disk, and fit warnings used in model catalog.

displays.label: Provider row
displays.description: Default, enabled, provider-level key requirement/source, status, last sync, model count, expanded model list, connect/remove controls.

displays.label: Subscription row
displays.description: Managed runtime id plus installed/signed-in/account-linked state, installed-source evidence, safe account labels, account launch controls, and status details only when the provider CLI exposes safe inspection or local non-token cache metadata; account-linked rows show the account in green with a status-not-live caveat. Sign out requires installed or linked evidence plus a reviewed logout command.

pages.body:
# Settings

Settings contains local desktop configuration for hardware, manager connection, coordinator status, self-update, managed subscription sign-ins, Ollama, image services, local stacks, agent chain RPCs, and inference backends.

## Use it for

- Fix manager connectivity or confirm the manager URL used by the app.
- Confirm the connected manager exposes the Control Center extension contract before relying on IDACC-only pages.
- Copy a manager compatibility report when a downloaded install is pointed at a stock, stale, or partially upgraded manager.
- Review and launch managed subscription CLI account flows for Claude, ChatGPT/Codex, Cursor, Grok, Antigravity, GitHub Copilot, Kiro, and legacy q when installed, while Perplexity API, Google Gemini API, xAI API, OpenRouter, NVIDIA, Groq API, and other metered provider accounts stay under Inference backends.
- Bring a new install into a driveable local path: download starter model, add Ollama backend, and sync models.
- Configure Agent chain RPCs whose enabled networks are mirrored into Identity as the chain allowlist for active granted keys.
- Check for, optionally auto-download, stage, and explicitly relaunch into app updates while pruning stale staged zips.

## Current guardrails

Coordinator is now read-only on Settings. The Connection card shows the active team's current coordinator and hands routing changes to HR Manager, where coordinator and primary-lead writes run through hierarchy fresh-reads and before/after previews. Settings no longer renders a separate first-run readiness strip. Manager compatibility checks, provider routing status, local runtime readiness, synced backend evidence, and guarded quick fixes now live in their owning cards; Re-check manager still repeats the compatibility read without changing manager state, and Copy manager report places the manager URL, connection state, required/reported API versions, extension id, feature coverage, and missing route signatures on the clipboard when diagnostics are needed. Provider default, enable/disable, remove, connect/sync, same-name add/replace, and local-stack backend creation now fresh-read the current provider list before writing. Connect & sync also sends the reviewed provider stamp into the bridge, and the bridge rechecks that stamp after the live probe before recording the model-list cache. Default promotion is blocked unless the backend is enabled, key-ready, and route-ready with a live, synced, or preset model list; the table disables stale default stars and the Local models strip only lists local providers that are actually route-ready. Disabling or removing the last enabled inference backend is blocked before and after confirmation. Same-name provider drafts now collapse to a disabled Configured state when the enabled endpoint is unchanged and no new hidden key is entered; real replacements still require an inline replace arm, compact before/after endpoint review, and a fresh provider recheck after confirmation. The provider picker now stays API/cloud-focused, and API-key helper text only appears for key-required API/cloud presets. Local stack backend fallback Add re-runs discovery and compares the current server snapshot before writing, while installed local provider stacks are reconciled into disabled pending backend rows and Scan running upgrades matching live presets returned by the fresh scan.

Agent chain RPC save, replace, remove, and check fresh-read the current endpoint row before acting; replacing an existing endpoint shows before/after URLs and Identity mirrors only the sanitized enabled RPC metadata. Local Ollama auto-backend creation rechecks current providers before adding the default Ollama backend. Local concurrency Apply fresh-reads the manager's current local inference limit before writing, and image-server Save/Clear fresh-reads the current preferred local image generator before and after review prompts before changing local-vs-API image routing. Local LLM stacks now own local backend setup and open on the backend-presets filter for Ollama, LM Studio, Jan, llama.cpp, LocalAI, and vLLM. The stack toolbar keeps all, backend presets, start here, easy, guided, and advanced as compact primary filters, while specialized tags such as GPU, privacy, Docker, and RAG sit behind an optional tag dropdown. Stack rows order install-ease tags as start here, easy, guided, advanced, and expert, show each setup note inline, show command-backed start-here/easy/guided/advanced paths as primary Install actions when no matching package/container evidence is detected, create pending backend rows when package/container evidence exists, and auto-upgrade matching live backend presets after Scan running confirms a real model-listing API. Command-backed guided/advanced stacks still review their install or start command before any Terminal run, and host-incompatible or unresolved-template commands stay as guided docs paths instead of opening a broken Terminal command. vLLM and Hugging Face TGI remain visible as Linux/GPU templates, but IDACC will not run their Linux-only or placeholder commands as one-click macOS installs. Uninstall actions now render only after read-only install evidence matches the expected uninstall path: Homebrew formula/cask, pip package, or Docker container as appropriate; clicking Uninstall rechecks that evidence before showing the command. Docker-backed commands check that Docker is installed and running before Terminal opens, and LocalAI shows Start when the local-ai container already exists but is not answering the scan. Existing Docker containers also report their mapped host API port, so a LocalAI container created on 8082 is scanned and added as 127.0.0.1:8082 instead of being treated as a broken 8080 install. Port evidence includes defaultPort, apiBase, Docker host mappings such as -p 8080:80, and explicit --port/--tcp flags, but rows now warn only on current-state evidence: live scan ports or already configured backend ports. Catalog-only shared defaults, such as multiple stack presets listing 8080, stay quiet until a real live/configured conflict exists. Running scan evidence and matching configured endpoints are warnings, not package-install authority; Remove backend stays separate from package removal. Terminal stack commands remain reviewed and explicit, with visible clipboard fallback when automation is blocked. Python-backed stack commands use `python3 -m pip` rather than a bare `pip` binary. MLX install pins `urllib3<2` for macOS system Python/LibreSSL, starts through the supported `python3 -m mlx_lm server` form, and exposes separate Start and Uninstall actions after package evidence is detected. LocalAI uses the CPU quickstart image for new Docker installs and routes existing containers through detached Start instead of rerunning docker run with the same name. LM Studio Start uses the installed lms server start command when available, so an app-open-but-server-off backend can become route-checkable without hunting through stack setup. Ollama install evidence now recognizes Homebrew formula, Homebrew cask, CLI, and app-bundle paths; stack rows also hide Install whenever a stack is already installed, running, or configured as a backend. Install or Start now schedules repeated fresh local scans and auto-adds any matching live stack backend, including alternate mapped ports, while installed-but-not-running stacks show package/container evidence without becoming routable. First-run local setup now keeps the manual backend add form focused on API/cloud providers, starts the local model pull field on qwen3:1.7b, and keeps manager extension compatibility, manager connectivity, explicit-vs-auto routing, synced local runtime readiness, ready backend counts, missing keys, and guarded quick actions inside the Connection, Local models & backends, Local LLM stacks, and Inference backends cards. The Local models & backends section now uses one compact status line for routing readiness, Ollama model count, installed local stacks, and catalog state; the header keeps only Check catalog, Scan running, and Stack setup actions. The old four-cell status grid and always-on Backend ready banner are gone. Local concurrency renders only when useful manager state exists, installed Ollama model chips stay passive except for reviewed removal, catalog digest changes surface a dedicated reviewed update row, and newly discovered public catalog tags flow directly into the searchable catalog without a separate added-tags tile. Local runtime readiness still means a local backend is synced/live rather than merely installed or configured, and the catalog includes the Gemma 4 MLX tags surfaced by Ollama's library. Managed subscription rows show safe account labels when a provider status command or non-token local cache exposes one; live CLI-confirmed rows say signed in, while cache-evidence rows say account linked and show the account in green with a status-not-live caveat. Grok reads email from its auth cache, Copilot reads the GitHub login from its managed config, and Antigravity can show a Google account-cache hint without treating cache-only evidence as runtime authority. Antigravity becomes assignable only when the manager exposes the Antigravity harness and Settings confirms live model access through `agy models`; stale linked evidence stays a re-check-needed row. Sign-out renders only after installed or linked account evidence exists and a safe provider logout command is known. Gemini CLI is no longer a managed subscription row; Google Gemini API setup lives in the Inference backends provider dropdown. Perplexity web account access similarly routes through the Perplexity API backend preset and API settings link rather than browser-session automation. Uninstalled legacy Amazon Q is hidden from the row list, and account-flow notices auto-expire; stack install/uninstall, model removal, model update, and provider removal remain explicit operator actions.

## Boundary

These settings are app-local configuration and infrastructure routing, not manager task data. Managed CLI rows may expose different levels of status detail: Claude, Codex, Cursor, Kiro, and legacy q can be inspected where their CLIs provide safe commands, while Grok, Antigravity, and Copilot keep account state inside their own TUI/device-flow surfaces after IDACC launches the account flow from Settings. IDACC may show local account labels from known non-token cache fields; these rows are account linked rather than signed in because the CLI did not provide live status. IDACC does not display provider tokens, RPC keys, or wallet/key secrets and does not fabricate logout buttons for CLIs that lack a reviewed logout command. Gemini API and Perplexity API belong to Inference backends; browser/OAuth cache or a web chat session by itself is not runtime authority. Coordinator/routing edits belong in HR Manager, not Settings. Local discovery snapshots must be fresh before provider writes; stack configured/running badges and claimed-port warnings are routing/runtime evidence, not proof that a package is installed or safe to uninstall. Claimed-port warnings are shown only for live scanned ports or already configured backend ports; catalog-only shared defaults are treated as setup context, not warning state. Stack uninstall does not silently remove backend routing, and backend removal does not uninstall packages; local stack auto-add and image-generator save/clear both recheck current infrastructure state before writing so a stale Settings dialog cannot silently replace newer routing. The API/Cloud provider dropdown lists remote or custom API backends; installable local LLM backend presets stay in Local LLM stacks, and experimental proxy routes such as Headroom stay out of everyday routing until a manager-backed core contract exists. Manager-extension diagnostics are read-only compatibility evidence for whether the connected id-agents manager includes the Control Center extension feature and route contract used by the downloaded app; they are surfaced only where the owning card needs them. Because release and update behavior is surfaced here, keep this page aligned with the package version and release process; staged update zips are cache files and should be pruned when stale or consumed, while GitHub releases remain the durable archive. The wiki drift check exists to catch stale release-facing documentation before shipping.

The latest pass makes local stack installs visible and routable without treating install evidence as runtime authority: Python-backed commands no longer depend on bare `pip`, Jan/GPT4All/MLX installs show package evidence, Install/Start auto-schedules guarded live scans that can add matching backends, alternate Docker host ports are respected, and Ollama models can be re-pulled from the installed model list or catalog. The Gemma 4 MLX catalog entries are included so Apple-Silicon operators can discover and refresh the new Ollama MLX variants from IDACC. Runtime pickers now keep subscription CLIs, the Ollama local model runtime, and API provider lanes separated; Claude Code's legacy local alias is preserved only for existing assignments, and Antigravity's assignability is driven by the live `agy models` adapter check.
implementedTwoHundredThirtySecondPass: Settings Local LLM stack installs now gate one-click Terminal actions on the current host platform, so Linux-only stacks such as Hugging Face TGI do not present a runnable Mac install button.
Stack install commands containing unresolved placeholders such as <model> are treated as guided setup templates instead of being opened in Terminal.
The Hugging Face TGI catalog note now describes it as a Linux/NVIDIA-oriented template that needs a real model id and compatible host before use.
Validation: npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki drift, diff check, and npm run build passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredThirtyThirdPass: Settings Local LLM stack install gating now derives the host platform from the browser/Electron runtime while hardware detection is still loading.
vLLM no longer exposes a transient one-click macOS install path before the hardware card finishes probing; it stays as a guided Linux/GPU setup template.
The vLLM catalog note now states that it should run on a compatible Linux GPU host with a real model id before adding the API backend to IDACC.
Validation: npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki drift, diff check, and npm run build passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredThirtyFourthPass: Settings Local LLM stack commands now check Docker installed/running state before opening Docker-backed installs, starts, or uninstalls in Terminal.
LocalAI install now rechecks the local-ai Docker container before docker run, so an existing stopped container becomes a Start/Uninstall path instead of a name-collision terminal error.
The LocalAI catalog now uses the CPU quickstart image for new Docker installs and exposes a docker start command for an installed container that is not yet live on the scanned API port.
Validation: npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki drift, diff check, and npm run build passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredThirtyFifthPass: Settings LocalAI triage now reads the existing local-ai Docker container state and mapped host port from docker inspect instead of assuming the catalog default port.
LocalAI Start now uses docker start local-ai so the container runs detached, and the delayed scan can discover the API after Terminal returns.
Installed LocalAI containers mapped to alternate host ports such as 8082 update the stack backend candidate to the detected port before Scan running or Add backend writes.
Validation: npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki drift, diff check, and npm run build passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredThirtySixthPass: Settings Local LLM stack commands now use python3 -m pip for Python-backed installs/uninstalls, and MLX starts via python3 -m mlx_lm.server so shells without a bare pip command do not fail immediately.
Settings now detects Jan, GPT4All, and MLX package install evidence and shows it separately from live-server routing authority.
Install and Start actions schedule follow-up local scans that auto-add matching live stack backends, including detected alternate host ports from Docker containers.
Local models & backends now exposes live local API chips, installed stack evidence, Ollama re-pull Update actions, and Gemma 4 MLX catalog entries.
implementedTwoHundredThirtySeventhPass: Settings Local models & backends now checks local stack install evidence immediately on Settings open, so Jan, GPT4All, MLX, and Docker-backed installs can appear before a manual Scan running action.
The card header separates Ollama model count from local stack install count instead of implying the section is Ollama-only.
Installed local stacks now render as visible chips with installed, running, or backend state, while live local APIs remain a separate routing-authority row.
View stack setup now jumps to Local LLM stacks with the backend-presets filter selected.
implementedTwoHundredThirtyEighthPass: Settings LM Studio setup now separates successful Homebrew app install from local server readiness: the stack row opens LM Studio and tells the operator to load or download a model, start the Local Server on port 1234, then Scan running.
Curated local stack commands allow macOS open handoffs for GUI-first stacks without treating an installed app as a routable backend until a model-listing API answers.
Validation: npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki JSON parse, and npm run build passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredThirtyNinthPass: Settings and Health now treat GitHub Copilot as assignable only after the id-agents manager exposes the new copilot harness adapter.
The runtime picker still leaves Grok Build, Google Antigravity, Kiro, and legacy q under linked subscription adapter-needed state until their manager adapters ship.
Validation: npm test in idctl, npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki JSON parse, and npm run build passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredFortiethPass: Settings and Health now treat Kiro as assignable only after the id-agents manager exposes the new kiro-cli harness adapter and Settings confirms a signed-in Kiro CLI account.
The Kiro runtime model catalog now shows Kiro's current model choices: auto, Claude Sonnet/Haiku, DeepSeek, MiniMax, GLM, and Qwen Coder options instead of a single default placeholder.
Validation: npm test in idctl, npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki JSON parse, and npm run build passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredFortyFirstPass: Settings and Health now treat Grok Build as assignable only after the id-agents manager exposes the new grok harness adapter and Settings confirms live Grok sign-in through `grok models`.
The Grok model catalog is populated from `grok models` when available, falling back to grok-composer-2.5-fast and grok-build if the CLI cannot be read.
Validation: npm test in idctl, npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki JSON parse, and npm run build passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredFortySecondPass: Settings and Health now treat Google Antigravity as assignable only after the id-agents manager exposes the new antigravity harness adapter and Settings confirms live sign-in/model access through `agy models`.
The Antigravity model catalog is populated from `agy models` when available, falling back to the current Gemini, Claude Thinking, and GPT-OSS model labels if the CLI cannot be read.
Validation: npm test in idctl, npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki JSON parse, and npm run build passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredFortyThirdPass: Legacy Amazon Q (`q`) is no longer promoted into Health/HR linked runtime lanes from installed/account evidence; Settings may still show the legacy row when installed, and existing q agent assignments remain visible through the current-runtime keep path.
This retires the last adapter-needed subscription lane from the default model picker without deleting compatibility for older q-assigned agents.
Validation: npm test in idctl, npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki JSON parse, and npm run build passed before release validation.

pages.purpose: Current audit record for controls that can rewrite live agent, task, project, capability, identity, provider, or prompt-dispatch state and therefore need review, confirmation, quality guards, or stronger state synchronization.
pages.scope: Documentation and implementation record for the current modified build. It records the first full dashboard-page audit, two hundred twelve conservative guardrail implementation passes, and the policy decisions still needed before more disruptive UX simplification.

tabs.label: Dashboard
tabs.description: Dashboard is now observe-first by default: command-palette project/org sync entries route to owner pages, the shortcuts drawer shows observe and owner-page handoffs only, duplicate direct project/org sync shortcuts have been removed, and primary-lead changes move to HR Manager. The hidden non-embedded Chat address panel also keeps coordinator marking read-only so it cannot bypass HR Manager if surfaced later.

tabs.label: Work
tabs.description: Task status, owner, redispatch, reconcile, create-work dispatch, plan status/dispatch, goal/loop/schedule/dream writes fresh-read live state before mutating. Work, Plans, and Learn all route user-needed blockers through questions:add, which now has store-level duplicate suppression: normalized scoped fingerprints and stable Learn keys fold repeated raises into one Inbox row with seenCount instead of duplicating questions.

tabs.label: Projects
tabs.description: Git actions, direct commit/push, auto-commit, repo create/link/fork, and pull actions can change repository or project-tracker state. Workspace sync now previews root/folder/add/adopt counts and rechecks the scan after confirmation before tracker writes; commit/push and pull preview branch/remote/status/diff/untracked files and recheck that git/diff stamp after confirmation, project-row writes fresh-read current tracker state, auto-commit mode changes sit behind an Automation disclosure, and background checkpoints only run when project and branch/remote state are exactly safe immediately before committing.

tabs.label: Health
tabs.description: Harness/model/effort/speed selectors stage local drafts and apply only after full diff review plus fresh fleet-row checks before and after confirmation; model options are derived from the effective staged harness catalog so stale cross-harness models render as drift instead of valid options. Probe all previews and rechecks the all-team running roster before probing each target in its own team; Start, reset session, stop, rebuild, delete, and row probe fresh-read the current row before changing context or process state. Lead stars are read-only hierarchy markers with an HR Manager handoff for coordinator routing. Runtime credential-lane cooldowns are surfaced as read-only manager state. Neutral provider model lanes show every configured subscription, local, and API backend without letting a provider id overwrite the manager runtime until a provider-runtime contract exists. Headroom is no longer a Health control; the hidden headroom:audit route reports why automatic prompt compression is not core-ready.

tabs.label: Identity & Keys
tabs.description: Controller proof gates privileged actions; renderer selection, proof cache, backend account IDs, Safe/session actions, and the Tauri local mock key store are team/name keyed. Identity actions fresh-read the selected agent row before the review prompt and again after confirmation; account/session-dependent actions also compare account or session stamps before writing. Legacy bare-name key records surface as review-only evidence instead of being auto-promoted.

tabs.label: HR Manager
tabs.description: Coordinator changes, default-only primary lead promotion, relay overrides, instruction saves with rebuild, Team Builder auto-wiring, selected-agent rebuild, team lifecycle/probe actions, empty-team delete, and org sync fresh-read current roster/config/hierarchy state before writing, probing, or rebuilding. Team ops previews exact targets and blocks if the roster changes after confirmation. Manage labels stale/non-running coordinator evidence and only running agents can be selected as current routing leads. Team Builder coordinator presets include the default coder/researcher validation return path, default/lead gets a distinct primary validation preset, Manage > Team ops owns lifecycle-only team ops, Structure owns selected-agent instructions, Route owns Relay/Hierarchy/org sync, shared harness labels match Health, the shared team-coordinator skill source teaches the same primary/secondary validation loop, and Build now has first-run recommended team blueprints.

tabs.label: Capabilities
tabs.description: MCP attach/detach, skill install/uninstall, and rebuild fresh-read selected targets before and after confirmation; registry add/remove recheck current catalogs, registry removal rechecks after confirmation, and skill delete verifies fresh all-team usage before removing records. Same-name MCP registry replacement now needs an inline replacement arm plus post-confirm registry recheck. The Skills tab now shows local-vs-Brain skill-index status, flags local create/delete/re-categorize drift, flags missing Brain /skills/index route/profile/facet/reuse/proposal fields as a stale catalog contract, reads Brain /fleet-report as a read-only fleet-authority chip, reads Brain /graph/app/data as a read-only graph-contract chip, and keeps additive Preview & sync Brain explicit and blocked when the Brain Skills contract is stale.

tabs.label: Computer Use
tabs.description: Arm, Bless, Remove, safety-mode changes, and Allow/Deny now fresh-read broker, pending-approval, attached-agent, or roster state before continuing. Empty Arm for permission testing requires an explicit no-blessed-agent review before Arm enables. Tokens, attached-agent rows, and the arm bless-list use team/name scoped authority; attach/detach require exact current ID/name matches and armed-list refreshes use attached-list stamps. Legacy bare-name tokens surface as review-only evidence and stay blocked by scoped arming.

tabs.label: Settings
tabs.description: Settings no longer renders a separate first-run readiness strip; manager compatibility, provider routing, local runtime readiness, synced backends, and guarded quick fixes now live in their owning cards, with copyable manager diagnostics still available when compatibility checks fail. Provider default/toggle/remove/connect/add, local discovery adds, EVM RPC writes, local concurrency, and image-server preference changes now fresh-read current settings before writing; provider Connect & sync is bridge-stamped before recording model lists. Same-name provider replacement needs an inline replacement arm before the final confirmation, while unchanged enabled endpoint drafts collapse to Configured without showing a replacement warning. Local discovery Add/Add all re-probe server snapshots and fresh provider lists before writing, and Add all rechecks the discovery snapshot again after review. Image-server Save/Clear also rechecks after review before changing local-vs-API image routing. Coordinator status is read-only here; HR Manager owns coordinator and primary routing edits. The last enabled inference backend cannot be disabled or removed. Local model readiness now distinguishes installed/configured from synced/default-ready, while the stack catalog labels configured/running evidence without offering inferred uninstall actions.

controls.label: Preview before apply
controls.description: Use for org sync, git commit/push, capability bulk apply, and hierarchy/routing changes. The preview should show target agents/teams, files or repos touched, and whether a rebuild or push will happen.

controls.label: Stage then apply
controls.description: Use for compact dropdown-heavy config surfaces such as Health harness/model/effort/speed. Draft changes locally, review the whole diff, block stale rows, and rebuild or write once per touched target. Keep provider/model catalog lanes read-only unless the manager exposes an executable provider-runtime contract.

controls.label: Fresh task preflight
controls.description: Use for task-card actions that mutate manager status, owner, or background dispatch. Refetch the task list plus lane/dependency overlays, block when status/owner/team/lane changed, and refresh before the operator tries again.

controls.label: Fresh plan preflight
controls.description: Use for brain-plan status writes, blocker-question creation, dispatch, and draft-plan promotion or deletion. Refetch live plan title/status/mtime or draft version/updatedAt/content before applying the action, and have the main writer reject stale expected brain-plan status snapshots.

controls.label: Fresh goal preflight
controls.description: Use for saved goal edits, AI refine saves, deletion, and driver metadata writes. Refetch the goal updatedAt marker before saving from an opened editor, and merge background driver metadata onto the latest saved goal instead of an older loaded copy.

controls.label: Fresh loop preflight
controls.description: Use for saved sequential loop chains. Keep the opened loop's updatedAt baseline, block save/run/schedule/delete when the saved chain changed, refresh the editor to the latest chain, and reload the saved record after each save so the baseline stays aligned.

controls.label: Fresh schedule preflight
controls.description: Use for heartbeat, schedule, check-in, and dream-schedule controls. Refetch current schedules or check-ins before update/pause/remove/close, block stale rendered rows, disclose and recheck Create Work interval-heartbeat replacements, and reject duplicate recurring Dream schedules before creating another calendar check-in.

controls.label: Fresh project preflight
controls.description: Use for project tracker writes and filesystem/git actions. Capture the opened project row, refetch before saving status/metadata/delete/auto-commit, re-anchor git actions to the current path, and recompute duplicate groups before bulk save/remove.

controls.label: Project automation disclosure
controls.description: Use for background project automation such as auto-commit. Keep the card row read-only with a state chip, move mode changes into an explicit Automation disclosure, and retain fresh-project preflight plus safe-state checkpoint rules before any write or commit request.

controls.label: Fresh agent preflight
controls.description: Use for Health roster actions and staged agent config writes. Refetch the all-team fleet row before lifecycle, row probe, lead, delete, and Apply actions; for Probe all, refetch the all-team running target set before and after confirmation; block if name/team/status/runtime/model/effort/speed/health/port or target set changed before writing or probing.

controls.label: Fresh identity preflight
controls.description: Use for Identity & Keys privileged actions. Refetch the selected team/agent row before challenge, verify, wallet, account, deploy, and issue actions; refetch the latest account/session row before revoke so stale session state cannot disable the wrong grant.

controls.label: Fresh HR preflight
controls.description: Use for HR Manager staffing and routing writes. Refetch the target roster, hierarchy, relay policy, org config, or instruction text before coordinator/default-primary changes, relay overrides, instruction save+rebuild, team lifecycle/probe actions, Team Builder post-build team-coordinator wiring, empty-team delete, and org sync apply. For team-wide operations, stamp the reviewed roster and block if it changes after confirmation.

controls.label: Focused HR modes
controls.description: Keep HR Manager owner panes narrow: Manage > Team ops owns lifecycle-only Team ops, Structure owns selected-agent instruction edits and membership moves, Route owns Relay plus Hierarchy/org sync, and Build owns roster creation.

controls.label: Coordinator instruction sync gate
controls.description: The central id-agents team-coordinator skill now matches the primary lead, secondary validator, parallel delegation, and goal-alignment policy. Deployed agent copies should be synced/rebuilt only after operator approval because that can touch live agent folders and processes.

controls.label: Downloadable team blueprints
controls.description: Keep first-run team creation useful even when the manager has no team library: IDACC provides safe spec-prefill blueprints for default leadership, operations, engineering, research, and onchain teams, and source changes reset stale roster drafts before parsing.

controls.label: Default primary validator preset
controls.description: Use when building or dispatching through default/lead. The primary lead delegates execution directly to team leads; default/coder and default/researcher validate completed work on the return path and judge accomplishments against the primary goal first, then secondary goals, then the objective.

controls.label: Runtime cooldown observation
controls.description: Use when the manager owns runtime credential lanes, cooldowns, rate-limit failover, provider recovery, or provider-runtime assignment. Fetch current cooldown and catalog state, display affected lanes/agents/providers as read-only operational truth, and avoid adding override or provider-assignment controls until manager-side lane policy exposes a guarded write API.

controls.label: Manager response drain
controls.description: Use for every shared manager HTTP client and desktop bridge poll surface. Bound normal and long-poll requests with explicit timeouts, coalesce duplicate in-flight read calls, and even on 4xx/5xx or graceful older-manager fallback paths, consume or cancel response bodies before throwing so Electron and TUI runtimes release sockets instead of accumulating CLOSE_WAIT or SYN_SENT connections.

controls.label: Manager keepalive budget
controls.description: Use for the id-agents manager HTTP server. Cap idle keepalive, headers wait, and requests per socket so stale or pre-fix dashboard clients cannot hold thousands of loopback sockets forever; apply through a manager rebuild/restart maintenance window.

controls.label: Fresh capability preflight
controls.description: Use for Capabilities target writes. Refetch every selected team/agent before and after confirmation, block moved or newly incompatible agents, compare current MCP/skill metadata, and pass each resolved target team explicitly before MCP, skill, or rebuild writes. For destructive catalog deletes, re-read registry state after confirmation and verify fresh all-team skill usage rather than trusting rendered counts.

controls.label: MCP registry replacement arm
controls.description: Use for same-name MCP server replacement. Detect catalog or custom drafts that collide with the rendered registry, show before/after endpoints, require Replace existing MCP server before enabling replacement, then fresh-read again after confirmation before mcp:add writes.

controls.label: Fresh Computer Use preflight
controls.description: Use for Computer Use Arm, Bless, Remove, safety-mode, and approval decisions. Refetch broker status, pending requests, attached agents, and selected roster rows before acting; recheck after confirmation when authority or rebuild state can change. Bridge-side attach/detach must require exact current agent id/name matches, and armed-list refreshes must include an attached-list stamp.

controls.label: Computer Use empty-arm review
controls.description: Use when Screen Recording is granted but no agents are blessed. Disable Arm until the operator explicitly acknowledges Start live view with no blessed agents; keep the broker preflight so a stale empty session cannot arm without review.

controls.label: Fresh Settings preflight
controls.description: Use for Settings provider, RPC, local concurrency, and image-server writes. Refetch the current settings row or manager value before writing, recheck after confirmation, and block stale replacement or last-enabled-backend mutations. For provider Connect & sync, carry the reviewed provider stamp through the bridge and recheck it after live probing before recording the model-list cache.

controls.label: Provider replacement arm
controls.description: Use for same-name inference backend replacement. Detect the rendered same-name provider in the add form, show before/after endpoints, require an explicit Replace existing backend arm before enabling the replace action, then still fresh-read and confirm before writing.

controls.label: Fresh read before full-list write
controls.description: Use for MCP server attach/detach and any API that posts a complete list. Refetch current agent metadata immediately before constructing the next list so stale UI state cannot erase another page's change.

controls.label: Team/id scoped authority
controls.description: Use for Identity & Keys and Computer Use so accounts, proofs, session grants, broker tokens, and blessed authority cannot collide when teams contain same-named agents. Legacy bare-name records should surface as review evidence until an explicit migration policy exists.

controls.label: Legacy authority review
controls.description: Use when older unscoped accounts, sessions, tokens, or permissions still exist. Show them as stale review artifacts, never as current authority, and require an operator policy before copying, revoking, or deleting them.

controls.label: Two-step destructive actions
controls.description: Use for reset session, rebuild active agents, provider removal, disabling the last usable backend, disabling Computer Use supervised mode, and stopping/removing recurring schedules.

controls.label: Observe-first Dashboard
controls.description: Keep Dashboard focused on observe, talk, refresh, and status. Move high-impact mutators such as primary-lead changes and org sync to HR Manager or require an explicit confirmation flow.

controls.label: Additive Brain catalog sync
controls.description: Before writing the local skill catalog to Brain, require safe Brain /health ok plus the current /skills/index route/profile/facet/reuse/no-store contract, fresh-read local SKILL.md entries, app auto-tags, and Brain summary, preview the additive write boundary, recheck local and Brain stamps after confirmation, and send a catalog stamp to the bridge.

controls.label: Brain fleet authority review
controls.description: Use when IDACC links to Brain dashboard Fleet/Health/Agents status. Read /fleet-report without writing, show exact manager-lifecycle running/total plus live/partial/cache/stale-contract authority, compare it against IDACC's live all-team store, and keep missing authority-label or count-drift states visible until Brain is restarted, redeployed, or scoped correctly. Health-ish labels such as online/ok stay in status breakdowns but must not inflate running totals.

controls.label: Brain graph contract review
controls.description: Use when IDACC links to Brain Graph. Read /graph/app/data without writing, verify neighbors=0 support, show expanded/direct graph counts, and keep missing source-authority contracts visible until Brain is restarted or redeployed.

controls.label: Explicit Brain replay
controls.description: Use when a Brain dashboard needs compute-heavy POST analysis such as eval replay or vector comparison. Keep passive GET reports on timers, but run replay only from an explicit operator refresh and show when it has not been run in the current view.

controls.label: Same-name agent telemetry
controls.description: Use when Brain dashboards combine live all-team fleet rows with legacy name-keyed telemetry. Keep the live row visible, but mark SLA, cost, probe, or bare controller-link fallbacks ambiguous when multiple current teams share the same agent name.

routing.decisionStatus: The audit findings are surfaced. One hundred one conservative guardrail passes are implemented; remaining simplification, live Brain redeploy, manager update strategy, and legacy-authority migration should wait for operator policy.
implementedFirstPass: Projects: stopped silent first-run workspace sync; commit/push, git pull, and enabling auto-commit now require explicit confirmation.
Projects: auto-commit remains opt-in per project and the automated checkpoint path reuses the reviewed enablement guardrail instead of prompting mid-background run.

Capabilities: default scope is now this team, broad or multi-agent applies require confirmation, MCP attach/detach refetches fresh agent metadata before posting a replacement list, and MCP registry removal confirms.
Computer Use: disabling supervised per-action approval now requires confirmation.
Settings: provider default changes, disabling enabled providers, and provider removal now require confirmation.
implementedSecondPass: Dashboard: drawer and command-palette project/org sync actions now confirm; primary lead selection routes by team/name and confirms before org sync.
Work: task lane status writes, owner reassignment, reconcile, redispatch, plan work/status changes, goal autopilot, schedules, loops, and dreams now confirm before live writes or dispatch.
Health: team lead, model/runtime/effort/speed, reset session, stop, and rebuild actions now confirm, with explicit active-agent warnings.

Identity & Keys: selected agent and controller proof state are team/name keyed in the renderer; wallet/account/session issue/deploy/revoke actions now confirm in addition to existing controller proof gates.

HR Manager: coordinator/primary changes, relay policy writes, per-agent relay overrides, instruction save+rebuild, selected-agent rebuild, Team Builder post-build wiring, org auto-sync toggles, and org sync now confirm.
implementedThirdPass: Identity & Keys: controller challenge messages, proof cache keys, key-provider account IDs, Safe/session deploy/issue/revoke calls, and the Tauri local mock key store now use team/name scoped authority.

Computer Use: attach/detach/attached methods route through the selected team, the MCP env carries ID_CU_TEAM and team/name ID_CU_AGENT authority, broker tokens store scoped authorities, and arm checks the same scoped bless-list.
Settings: coordinator changes now confirm, and disabling or removing the last enabled inference backend is blocked.
implementedFourthPass: HR Manager: Org sync now has an org:preview dry-run that uses the same sidecar change detection as org:sync and reports scanned agents, changed goal sidecars, planned brain write, planned rebuilds, rebuild cap, deferred rebuilds, and affected team/agent rows before apply.
HR Manager: Org sync task-busy and query-busy rebuild deferrals are now scoped by team where manager data exposes team names, reducing same-name cross-team rebuild ambiguity.
implementedFifthPass: Projects: manual commit/push now previews folder, branch, remote, compare target, ahead/behind state, dirty state, tracked diff stat, untracked files, and commit subject before staging, committing, or pushing.
Projects: pull now previews folder, branch, remote, compare target, ahead/behind state, dirty state, tracked diff stat, and untracked files before running the resilient pull.
Projects: manual commit exits early when the preview sees no working changes, avoiding a no-op commit attempt.
implementedSixthPass: HR Manager: coordinator changes now preview the previous coordinator and current primary before writing.
HR Manager: primary lead promotion now previews old primary, new primary, and affected team coordinator before writing.
HR Manager: team relay policy and per-agent relay overrides now preview previous and next delegation policy before writing.
implementedSeventhPass: Health: fleet lead, model, runtime, effort, speed, reset-session, stop, and rebuild confirmations now include current agent status plus before/after fields or effect summaries.

Health: Headroom pilot route, sampling, context threshold, workspace state, state-root, and safety-mode edits are staged locally; Apply policy previews the diff before writing, and Refresh asks before discarding unsaved policy edits.
Health: the Tauri adapter now honors the selected row's team for remote, probe, runtime, effort, and speed calls so all-team roster actions do not fall back to the active team.
implementedEighthPass: Dashboard: command-palette project/org sync commands now open Projects or HR Manager instead of mutating tracker or org state directly.
Dashboard: shortcuts drawer now defaults to observe actions and owner-page handoffs.
Dashboard: primary fleet-lead changes are no longer exposed as a Dashboard selector because HR Manager owns the routing diff preview.
implementedNinthPass: Projects: background auto-commit checkpoints now preflight git status and diff before drafting or committing.

Projects: auto-commit skips to manual review when the repo has no remote, unknown compare state, an upstream-gone branch, any behind commits, any ahead commits, unavailable diff data, or no working changes.
Projects: auto-commit enablement copy and tooltip now state that checkpoints only commit and push when the branch is exactly in sync with its remote.
implementedTenthPass: Identity & Keys: mock key providers now expose a read-only legacy authority report for old bare-name accounts and sessions that match current scoped agents.

Identity & Keys: the renderer shows a Legacy Authority Review card with account, deployment, active-session, non-expiring-session, and target scoped-authority details instead of silently promoting legacy key records.
Computer Use: the broker now exposes a secret-free legacy token report for old bare-name per-agent tokens that match current scoped agents.
Computer Use: the Who can drive panel now surfaces old bare-name tokens as blocked-by-scoped-arming review evidence instead of treating them as active authority.
implementedEleventhPass: Health: runtime, model, effort, and speed dropdowns now stage local config drafts instead of writing immediately.
Health: Apply changes previews the combined diff, verifies the live agent row still matches the staged name/status/runtime/model/effort/speed baseline, and blocks stale drafts before any write.
Health: applying staged config writes each changed field and rebuilds each touched agent once; Start/Rebuild is blocked while that agent has an unapplied config draft.
implementedTwelfthPass: Work: task lane drag/drop now refetches live tasks, lane overlays, and dependency overlays before writing a lane overlay or mapped manager status, blocking stale status/owner/team/lane cards.
Work: owner assignment now refetches the task and blocks if live status, owner, or team changed before overwriting ownership.
Work: individual and batch re-dispatch now fresh-read the task before reassigning or sending a new background request; stale batch rows are skipped and the board refreshes.
implementedThirteenthPass: Work Plans: Work, blocker scan, dispatch, Set pending, and Mark done fresh-read live brain-plan title/status/mtime before writing status, creating decision questions, or delegating work.
Work Plans: the main brain-plan status writer accepts an expected status/mtime snapshot and rejects stale writes if README status or plan-file mtime changed after review.
Work Plans: draft plan AI updates, promotion into brain plans, lifecycle status changes, and two-step delete now preflight the opened draft version/updatedAt/content before saving or removing.
implementedFourteenthPass: Work Goals: field edits, status changes, Autopilot toggles, AI refine saves, and two-step delete now refetch the opened goal updatedAt marker and block if the saved goal changed.
Work Goals: goal-driver config changes merge with freshly loaded driver config, and Autopilot enable/run-now prompts show the current global active Autopilot goal count and task cap.
Goal driver: background driver metadata saves now merge onto the latest saved goal record so driver notes cannot restore stale title/status/content from the copy loaded at run start.
Work Goals: removed the duplicate New Goal agent label so the form presents one clear agent row.
implementedFifteenthPass: Work Goals: after goal save/refine/field edit, the editor reloads the saved goal record so its freshness marker matches the store-written updatedAt value.
Work Loops: opening a saved chain now records a baseline and save/run/schedule operations block if that saved chain changed before the action applies.
Work Loops: run and schedule persist run metadata or schedule-ready definitions onto the freshly checked chain instead of an older opened copy.
Work Loops: saved-chain delete checks the rendered updatedAt marker and refreshes instead of deleting a chain that changed after the list rendered.
implementedSixteenthPass: Work Schedule: heartbeat enable/update now refetches current heartbeat rows before creating the replacement and pruning old schedules.
Work Schedule: heartbeat pause/resume/remove and other-team heartbeat controls refetch the schedule row and block if active state, interval, targets, or message changed.
Work Schedule: individual check-in close and stale-check-in cleanup refetch current check-in state and block when the active/stale set changed.
Work Dream: nightly dream scheduling checks for an existing 03:00 daily dream schedule for the same team/agent and passes the team explicitly when creating one.
Work Dream: dream report delete refetches the report and blocks if it disappeared or changed since the list rendered.
implementedSeventeenthPass: Projects: edit/save captures the opened project baseline, fresh-reads the tracker row before saving metadata, and preserves existing auto-commit settings during edits.
Projects: status changes, two-step delete, and auto-commit mode changes now refetch the current project row before writing.
Projects: manual commit, AI commit drafting, git actions, open-folder, and background auto-commit checkpoints re-anchor to the current project path before touching the filesystem.
Projects: repo create/link checks the project row before the GitHub side effect, then merges the resulting repo link into the latest tracker row instead of overwriting newer metadata.
Projects: duplicate combine recomputes duplicate groups from a fresh list, previews the group names, and rechecks the group fingerprint before save/remove.
implementedEighteenthPass: Health: staged runtime/model/effort/speed Apply now refetches the all-team fleet before the review prompt and again immediately before writing.
Health: team lead changes refetch the agent row and current coordinator hierarchy before confirming and writing coordinator routing.
Health: Start, Stop, Rebuild, Reset session, Probe, and Delete refetch the current agent row before issuing remote lifecycle commands.
Health: Start now confirms like the other lifecycle mutators, and Delete uses the richer status/effect confirmation instead of a bare one-line prompt.
Health: direct Probe buttons now route through the same fresh-agent preflight as the action menu.
implementedNineteenthPass: Identity & Keys: New challenge and Verify signature now fresh-read the selected all-team agent row before using controller wallet evidence.
Identity & Keys: Provision wallet, Register identity, Create account, Deploy account, and Issue scoped key fresh-read the selected agent row before mutating key or identity state.
Identity & Keys: Deploy also refreshes account state and blocks if the account was already deployed after the card rendered.
Identity & Keys: Revoke session key fresh-reads both selected agent state and the latest session row, blocking if the grant disappeared, changed, or was already revoked.
Identity & Keys: privileged button handlers now route through the guarded action functions instead of using stale selected name/team arguments captured from the rendered card.
implementedTwentiethPass: HR Manager: instruction Save & rebuild fresh-reads the target team/agent row and the current saved instruction text before writing, then rebuilds only that current agent.
HR Manager: selected-agent rebuild, move, empty-team delete, team relay, and per-agent relay controls fresh-read the relevant roster or policy row before prompting and writing.
HR Manager: coordinator and primary-lead changes fresh-read current hierarchy plus target agent existence before showing the routing diff prompt.
HR Manager: team lifecycle/probe actions now use one current-state confirmation with fresh running/total counts, sample targets, verified roster state, and post-confirm roster-stamp blocking.
HR Manager: Team Builder fresh-reads the target team roster before onboarding and rechecks hierarchy/relay stamps before post-build coordinator or relay auto-wiring.
HR Manager: org auto-sync toggles and Preview & sync fresh-read org config and hierarchy; sync apply blocks if org state changed after preview.
implementedTwentyFirstPass: Capabilities: MCP attach/detach, skill install/uninstall, and target rebuild now fresh-read every selected target before the confirmation prompt and again immediately before writing.
Capabilities: target preflight blocks moved agents, newly incompatible runtimes, and stale MCP/skill metadata, then passes each resolved target team explicitly to manager methods.
Capabilities: MCP attach/detach still constructs the replacement MCP server list from the fresh metadata row so wholesale MCP writes cannot erase newer attachments.
Capabilities: adding an MCP profile with an existing name now fresh-reads the registry and requires an explicit replace confirmation with before/after endpoint details.
Capabilities: MCP registry removal rechecks the registry after confirmation, and library skill deletion verifies fresh all-team usage before removing records.
implementedTwentySecondPass: Computer Use: Arm fresh-reads broker status and current attached agents before and after confirmation, blocking if the broker was armed or the blessed list changed during review.

Computer Use: Bless fresh-reads the selected active-team agent row and MCP metadata before and after confirmation, blocks moved/changed/incompatible targets, and re-syncs the armed blessed list from current attachments after a successful rebuild.

Computer Use: Remove fresh-reads the attached scoped authority before and after confirmation, detaches only the current row, rebuilds, and refreshes the armed blessed list if the broker is already armed.
Computer Use: Allow/Deny fresh-reads the pending request and blocks stale, changed, expired, or already-resolved approvals before releasing an action.
Computer Use: Pause, Disarm, and PANIC remain immediate stop paths and are no longer disabled by long attach/rebuild work; supervised-mode changes fresh-read broker mode before writing.
implementedTwentyThirdPass: Settings: provider default, enable/disable, remove, connect/sync, and same-name add/replace fresh-read the current provider row before writing; provider Connect & sync now rechecks a bridge-side provider stamp after probing before writing model-list metadata.
Settings: disabling or removing the last enabled inference backend is blocked using the fresh provider list before and after confirmation.
Settings: discovered local-server Add and Add all new re-read the provider list, re-run discovery for bulk add, de-dupe by normalized base URL, and confirm the current bulk-add target list.
Settings: EVM RPC save/replace/remove/check fresh-read the current endpoint row before writing or probing, with before/after URL review for replacement.

Settings: local concurrency Apply fresh-reads the manager's current limit, image-server Save/Clear fresh-reads the current preference before changing local-vs-API image routing, and Ollama auto-backend creation rechecks current providers before adding.
implementedTwentyFourthPass: Brain dashboard: /fleet-report now prefers live all-team manager polling over cached Brain agent entities.
Brain dashboard: Fleet and Agents tabs label live-manager versus cache fallback source and surface cache-drift warnings when Brain memory counts disagree with the live manager.
Brain dashboard: regression coverage verifies that live manager totals win over stale cached agent entities.
implementedTwentyFifthPass: HR Manager: fleet-primary promotion is locked to the default team in Structure and Manage controls, while non-default teams can still choose team coordinators.

HR Manager: Team Builder post-build auto-wiring now calls coordinator:set for the target team instead of coordinator:setPrimary, so building an ops/research/engineering team cannot overwrite default-team primary authority.
HR Manager: Reactive org-sync copy now describes completed work flowing back through default-team validators, coder and researcher, before returning to the primary lead.
implementedTwentySixthPass: HR Manager: generic and roster-aware Team Builder coordinator presets now include a validation return path through default/coder and default/researcher before work is treated as final.
HR Manager: coordinator presets now tell team leads to ask coder for implementation/operations/code-quality validation and researcher for evidence/reasoning/sourcing/policy/completeness validation.
HR Manager: bounced validator feedback now routes back to the responsible teammate or team lead for another refinement cycle instead of letting unvalidated summaries reach default/lead.
implementedTwentySeventhPass: Settings: Local LLM stacks now carry install-ease labels so starter paths are distinguishable from advanced serving engines.
Settings: the local-stack shortlist now orders Ollama, LM Studio, Jan, GPT4All, llama.cpp, MLX, LocalAI, Docker Model Runner, vLLM, and TGI for a download-and-drive operator path.
Settings: reviewed stack install commands now show visible Terminal-open or clipboard-fallback status instead of failing silently when Terminal automation is blocked.
implementedTwentyEighthPass: Capabilities: Skills now displays Brain /skills/index status beside the local catalog.
Capabilities: Preview & sync Brain additively upserts local SKILL.md definitions into Brain graph nodes and writes a shared catalog memory.
Capabilities: Brain skill sync is explicit and non-destructive; it does not delete Brain-only learned skill nodes or change agent-installed skills.
implementedTwentyNinthPass: Brain dashboard Health: approval Approve, Reject, and Apply actions now fresh-read /approvals/:id before acting and block if the queue item changed status or disappeared.
Brain dashboard Health: Apply now confirms the exact approval kind and subject before mutating Brain memory, facts, instructions, or graph identity.
Brain dashboard Health: confirmation-required backend responses are handled by prompting for the required phrase and retrying explicitly instead of failing opaquely from a stale tile.
implementedThirtiethPass: Brain dashboard Learning: passive load and 30-second refresh now read learning report, metrics, and pending approvals only.
Brain dashboard Learning: eval replay and vector comparison now run only through an explicit Refresh eval replay button.
Brain dashboard Learning: route comparison starts with a visible not-run state and records the in-view replay run time or error instead of hiding recurring POST work behind dashboard polling.
implementedThirtyFirstPass: Brain dashboard Agents: duplicate current agent names across teams now keep live status/model/runtime visible but hold SLA, cost, probe-failure, and bare controller-link fallbacks as ambiguous.
Brain dashboard Agents: controller lookup now prefers strong scoped ids and refuses bare name or agent:name fallback when the same name exists in multiple teams.
Brain dashboard Graph: the graph app now states that filters, refresh, and JSON use read-only GET /graph/app/data and do not write Brain state.
recommendedFirstPass: Projects: decide whether auto-commit should remain as an explicit per-project feature or be removed from the UI.
Settings: decide whether same-name inference provider replacement should stay allowed with explicit before/after confirmation or require unique backend names.
Computer Use: decide whether Arm with no blessed agents should stay allowed after confirmation for live-view permission testing or be blocked until at least one agent is blessed.

One-hundred-fifty-seventh guardrail pass normalizes the Capabilities plugin catalog and adds guarded instruction-plugin digestion into SKILL.md.
One-hundred-fifty-eighth guardrail pass hides digested instruction plugins from Plugins and simplifies the tab into a compact active-package list.
One-hundred-fifty-ninth guardrail pass simplifies MCP and Skills headers while keeping MCP list/test read-only in the sync bus.
One-hundred-sixtieth guardrail pass demotes Brain-wide review notices out of the Skills tab while preserving guarded Brain launch checks.

# Sync & Guardrails Audit

This page records the ongoing pass over Dashboard, Work, Projects, Health, Identity & Keys, HR Manager, Capabilities, Computer Use, Settings, and the Brain dashboard tabs. It now documents two hundred eighteen conservative guardrail implementation passes.

## Current build state

IDACC is sync-bus aware across dashboard, work, projects, health, plans, goals, loops, schedules, dreams, agent capability changes, and org sync. The guardrail layer favors fresh reads, preview prompts, scoped team/name authority, owner-page handoffs, locked default leadership roles, team-scoped org-instruction authority, effective-primary HR aligned Health model-lane grid, managed subscription account-label probes, linked-account status copy cleanup, managed subscription auto-refresh, API-focused Settings backend picker, local-stack scan
