quiet local-stack port guardrails, command-backed Local LLM stack install CTAs, installed-only managed subscription sign-out controls, Gemini API backend placement, Antigravity non-assignable account setup, hidden uninstalled legacy-q rows, expiring subscription notices, merged HR Manage operations, and an observe-only Dashboard drawer before mutation, and hierarchy-resolved Dashboard lead chat across all teams, and hierarchy-resolved Work cross-team dispatch/triage targets, hierarchy-resolved Projects AI helper routing plus truthful direct-commit labels, and Health default-backbone delete lockouts plus token-throughput cleanup and Headroom UI demotion.

The previous pass audited Headroom for core token savings and kept it out of the Health frontend. That pass exposed a hidden headroom:audit route reporting coreReady:false until reversible retrieval, manager retrieval handles, per-dispatch audit records, and protected-content fallback gates exist; at that point Work, Chat, Plans, Learn, and validator prompts stayed direct so quality and contextual understanding were not silently degraded.

The previous pass refactored Health token throughput and Headroom pilot policy. Token usage now refreshes independently from the 3s fleet poll, exposes recent sample versus 24h/7d averages plus top agent/model summaries, and Headroom now has a first-class readiness checkpoint that fresh-reads CLI/proxy status before policy writes without wiping unsaved edits on roster refresh.

The previous pass hardened Inbox blocker de-duplication. The question store now normalizes and fingerprints incoming blocker questions by team plus task/plan/learn scope, folds exact or high-overlap repeats into the existing row, merges options, and renders a raised-count marker so repeated scans do not create duplicate Inbox rows.

The previous pass refactored Work > Plans status automation. Live Brain plans now use one guarded Pending/Partial/Paused/Done status path, show lifecycle counts, expose Work next automation, pause on blocker questions, mark delegated plans Partial, and keep synthetic plan blocker refs out of task review overlays.

The previous pass added Work > Learn material ingestion plus restored Inbox as a top-level decision queue. Learn stores submitted GitHub/site snapshots, imported PDFs, and guarded folder extracts in IDACC storage, compares only against active goals, mirrors material state to Brain timeline/memory, routes safe digest packets to active team leads, and keeps tasks, goals, feature updates, and blocker questions behind review gates.

The previous pass closed the Work goal instruction-sync gap. Work `goals:save` and `goals:remove` now sync active Work goals into Brain team-instruction memory and immediately run no-rebuild Org Sync, so agent `.id-instructions.md` sidecars update when goals are added, updated, activated, deactivated, or removed from Work.

The previous pass added hidden deterministic context budgeting for token savings. Bridge `dispatch`, `remote`, `dispatch:start`, and Work automation `/ask` paths now inspect prompts before delivery; eligible oversized prompts can have whitespace, exact duplicates, or marked background sections compacted, while secrets, auth material, instruction sidecars, active code patches, wallet/key material, low-context prompts, and low-savings prompts stay exact. Optimized dispatches write local 0600 hash/redacted-preview audit records and hidden report routes; no frontend page renders a token-savings toggle.

The previous pass hardened context-budget secret exposure and added the testing phase. Context-budget records no longer persist raw original or sent commands; hidden record/recent/dry-run routes return redacted bounded previews plus hashes, token counts, transforms, and redaction metadata, and legacy raw-command audit files from the prior build are sanitized on first context-budget access. The protected-content detector now covers common bearer/API/GitHub/Slack/AWS/Google/JWT patterns plus seed phrases and instruction sidecars. `npm run test:context-budget` verifies eligible savings, protected direct fallback, redacted dry-run output, and reports aggregate estimated savings for review.

The previous pass adds persistent context-budget measurement. Every inspected `/ask` decision now updates hidden aggregate counters in `context-budget/stats.json`, grouped by day, source, team, route, transform, and protected-content class. The file stores counts and estimated token totals only, never commands, prompts, previews, secrets, auth material, or wallet/key text. `context:budgetReport` and `headroom:audit` now include session plus persisted all-time/today/7-day savings views so operators can compare estimated real-use savings over time while keeping the frontend quiet.

The previous pass validates the next Headroom/backend contract path without activating retrieval routing. `context:budgetReplayChats` can dry-run saved local chat prompts as an aggregate-only historical corpus, with no manager contact, no prompt persistence, and no raw chat text in the report. `headroom:backendContract` now names the minimal-change path: keep stock managers on deterministic/direct routing, prototype an optional `idacc-context-retrieval` plugin through the existing id-agents plugin attachment flow, and require `/capabilities` retrieval-contract evidence plus resolve/hash/expiry/fallback smoke tests before IDACC sends retrieval handles.

The previous pass validates the plugin path without letting it pigeonhole the platform. IDACC now bundles an `idacc-context-retrieval` candidate plugin with manifest, resolver instructions, and a contract tool that smoke-tests store/resolve, source-hash verification, expiry, and protected-content rejection. The hidden `headroom:pluginPath` report marks the plugin candidate pilot-ready but keeps `coreReady:false` for plugin-only routing because id-agents plugins cover Claude-family runtimes only; Headroom as core compression still needs MCP or manager-advertised retrieval support plus direct fallback so Codex, Ollama, cursor, Brain memory, goals, plans, tasks, routing, keys, and wallets remain usable.

The previous pass makes the plugin path credibly runtime-neutral at the IDACC package layer. The bundled `idacc-context-retrieval` manifest now declares Skill, MCP, native Claude plugin, and direct-fallback adapters; `headroom:pluginPath` reports portable-plugin runtime coverage separately from native plugin coverage, and `npm run test:headroom-plugin` smoke-tests MCP tool listing/calls without exposing protected content. Native plugin loaders remain runtime-specific, but IDACC portable plugin packages can cover Codex, Ollama, cursor-cli, Claude-family runtimes, and future runtimes through declared adapters plus direct fallback.

The previous pass refactors Capabilities so runtime neutrality is reflected in base UI wiring, not only Headroom audit output. MCP, Skills, and Plugins no longer use native runtime support as a hard target-selection gate; every local/API/subscription runtime in scope can receive capability assignment, while the UI labels native MCP, Skill, native plugin, MCP+Skill, Skill+fallback, and fallback/advisory execution surfaces. The Plugins tab now always lists the bundled `idacc-context-retrieval` portable package even when manager native plugin inventory is empty, and shows selected-target reach for native, MCP, Skill, and fallback adapters.

The previous pass normalizes the Capabilities plugin catalog. IDACC now fresh-reads plugin detail metadata through the existing manager route, returns sanitized structural inspection data to the renderer, classifies portable packages, instruction-only SKILL.md wrappers, hybrid tool packages, native tool plugins, and manifest-only inventory, and lets instruction-only plugins be digested into the local SKILL.md catalog only after a fresh-read confirmation. Tool-bearing packages such as SkillMesh remain plugin/adapter-owned so tool calls, environment requirements, keys, and runtime assumptions are not flattened into plain instructions. Plugin digestion writes through the existing create-skill route, refuses same-name catalog conflicts, marks Brain catalog review needed, and leaves Brain sync explicit.

The previous pass simplifies the Plugins tab into an active-package list. Digested instruction wrappers are filtered out once the same-named SKILL.md exists, the header shows Active and In Skills counts, the neutral-contract explainer was removed, and each row is reduced to package, kind, reach, and action so Plugins stays focused on packages that still need adapter/runtime handling.

The previous pass simplifies MCP and Skills without weakening their guards. MCP now uses a compact Servers/Attached header, a server/status/action table, and an explicit Add server reveal; `mcp:list` and `mcp:test` no longer emit cross-page sync invalidations. Skills uses a compact Brain skill status chip instead of separate catalog/core/fleet/agents/graph header chips, while the existing Brain dashboard launch guards, fresh-read install/delete guards, and explicit Preview & sync Brain boundary remain intact.

The latest pass refactors the Brain popout and dashboard tabs into read-only observation surfaces. /fleet-report now carries an IDACC authority block plus redacted SkillMesh/public identity and advertised-skill summaries; Health and Learning remove dashboard-side mutation/replay controls; Skills labels itself as an IDACC-synced additive graph view; Agents shows per-agent SkillMesh address and skill counts from the redacted fleet contract; and Graph sanitizes entity data while using live /fleet-report overlays only for unambiguous lifecycle, SkillMesh, and capability detail.

The latest pass aligns Brain Agents with Identity & Keys controller-wallet precedence and adds per-agent total-vs-last-24h ETH gas spend. /fleet-report now exposes a redacted controllerWallet summary, an onchain gas summary per agent, and a fleet onchainGas aggregate sourced only from Brain timeline transaction/gas events matched by agent identity or controller wallet; the dashboard renders those fields read-only and treats missing gas events as unknown or none recorded, not proof of zero chain cost.

The latest pass expands managed runtime options without flattening API providers into subscription sign-ins. Settings now treats `grok`, `gemini`, `copilot`, `kiro-cli`, and legacy `q` as managed CLI runtime lanes alongside Claude, Codex, and Cursor; visible install/sign-in actions run through provider CLIs, status is shown only where safe status commands exist, and Perplexity/xAI API-style accounts remain Inference backend provider lanes. The runtime catalog and bundled portable plugin coverage now include those managed runtimes through Skill/MCP/direct-fallback adapters while native plugin loading remains Claude-family only.

The latest pass cleans up Settings install-state handoffs. Managed subscription installers now auto-recheck installed binary/app evidence, show installed-source detection, keep sign-in/opening as a separate explicit action, and call out the Kiro vendor installer behavior that may open the app once. Local LLM stack rows now distinguish installed, running, and backend-added states, poll install evidence after Terminal handoff, and expose Add backend only after a fresh scan confirms a live local server.

The latest pass fixes Settings self-update behavior. The main updater now owns launch/background checks without a renderer duplicate, auto-download stages a found update while applying it requires the explicit Restart & update action, stale staged metadata and older update zips are pruned during startup/checks, and the apply helper removes the consumed update zip after a successful bundle swap.

The previous pass removed automatic unmanaged terminal launches for TUI-owned managed runtimes. Grok, Gemini, and GitHub Copilot rows used a passive detection-only state until the managed account-launch contract was added; status-inspectable providers kept their explicit sign-in/sign-out controls.

The latest pass gates Dashboard/Health Harness and HR Manager team-builder runtime pickers on Settings availability. Runtime choices now come from signed-in status-inspectable managed CLIs, installed presence-only managed CLIs, live/synced API backends with manager harnesses, and synced local providers; existing agent assignments stay visible as the current value, but unavailable catalog runtimes are no longer offered for new selection.

The latest pass clarifies Health token throughput as manager-reported telemetry instead of provider billing truth. Last-turn samples only drive the gauge while fresh; stale samples stay visible as context, while the gauge falls back to the 24h average so an old high tok/s record cannot look live.

The latest pass brings TUI-owned managed subscription runtimes back under the IDACC Settings control surface. Grok, Gemini, and GitHub Copilot rows now offer IDACC-launched Manage account flows instead of using the legacy outside-account label; Grok also exposes IDACC-launched logout while rows still distinguish vendor CLIs that do not expose safe non-interactive status.

The latest pass re-syncs Health Fleet model lanes with Settings runtime availability. runtime:freshness now marks only Settings-proven harnesses as selectable, the Fleet panel hides unavailable curated fallback harnesses unless an agent is already assigned to them, assigned-but-unavailable rows render as current-only review state, and Gemini CLI oauth-personal evidence is treated as needs-review rather than assignable because browser OAuth can succeed while the CLI project auth step fails.

The latest pass cleans up Settings Local LLM stacks. The old all-tags chip cloud is replaced by compact primary filters, a single advanced tag dropdown, a quieter count/status line, and a right-aligned Scan running action so the section leads with installable stack rows instead of filter noise.

The latest pass fixes managed subscription sign-out visibility. Settings now requires installed CLI evidence before rendering Sign out, so uninstalled Kiro or legacy Amazon Q rows cannot offer logout just because their catalog metadata includes a logout command.

The latest pass triages Gemini CLI consumer OAuth deprecation. Settings keeps Google Antigravity CLI as the managed consumer subscription successor without offering it as an agent harness until the manager exposes an adapter, while Google Gemini API setup belongs to Inference backends.

The latest pass reduces Settings managed-sign-in noise. The legacy Amazon Q row now renders only when installed evidence exists, the long explanatory footer under managed sign-ins is gone, and account-flow notices auto-expire so old Kiro/Grok messages do not linger after the operator has moved on.

The latest pass removes Gemini CLI from managed subscription sign-ins. The Google Gemini API preset now lives solely in Inference backends under API / Cloud, with clearer dropdown labeling and notes, and the managed-runtime status feed no longer promotes Gemini CLI env/API evidence into assignable availability.

The latest pass fixes Health harness/model sync. AgentTable now derives model choices from the effective staged harness, resets invalid staged models to the selected harness catalog when Harness changes, and shows persisted cross-harness model drift as a choose-model warning instead of offering stale models as valid options.

The latest pass adds safe managed-subscription account labels and linked-account sign-out gating. Settings now surfaces account labels from status commands or non-token local cache metadata for Grok, Antigravity account hints, GitHub Copilot, and existing status-inspectable CLIs; Sign out appears only when installed or linked evidence exists and the provider has a reviewed logout command, so Copilot and Antigravity do not get fake logout buttons.

The latest pass cleans up the visual semantics for managed subscription account state. Settings now explains signed in as live CLI-confirmed status and account linked as safe local account evidence without a live status command; linked rows render the account in green and reserve muted text for the status-not-live caveat.

The latest pass adds Settings managed subscription auto-refresh. The card now refreshes account status on Settings open, window focus/visibility return, and every five minutes while mounted, and it warms runtime model/freshness routes so new provider, Codex, or local model evidence can surface without a manual Re-check. Re-check remains an immediate manual refresh, and vendor CLI package installs/upgrades are still explicit reviewed actions rather than silent background mutation.

The latest pass focuses Settings inference backend setup on API/cloud providers and moves local backend creation into Local LLM stacks. The provider picker now starts with API/cloud presets and custom API kinds only, while Local LLM stacks reconciles installed provider stacks into disabled pending backend rows, and Scan running upgrades matching live presets after the fresh local API scan proves a real model-listing endpoint. Pending installed stacks remain non-routable until Connect & sync succeeds, row-level Add backend stays as a fresh-scan fallback, and local package install/uninstall remains separate from backend routing cleanup.

The previous pass added Perplexity API setup support without treating the browser subscription session as a routable runtime. The provider catalog explains the API-key boundary, and key resolution recognizes PERPLEXITY_API_KEY/PPLX_API_KEY in addition to IDCTL_PERPLEXITY_API_KEY. Perplexity remains an API backend for agent routing until an official safe CLI/runtime status contract exists.

The latest pass cleans up Health/Fleet model lanes into a column-aligned grid. Runtime name, lane type, model count, source/freshness, and checked time now render in fixed columns with ellipsis instead of loose row actions, so long provider names cannot shove harness/source chips out of alignment.

The latest pass triages the Settings Local image generator card. The card now presents local image generation as an optional local-first preference, points fallback to image-capable API backends instead of a hardcoded OpenRouter path, uses Scan local with a single amber no-server result, widens the API-style selector, and separates local preference from fallback status so normal unconfigured localhost state does not look like an app failure.

The latest pass merges HR Manager Manage into Route. HR Manager now renders only Structure, Build, and Manage at the top level; Manage contains Team ops, Overview, Relay, and Hierarchy. Former lifecycle controls live under Manage > Team ops, Structure start/stop handoffs point there, and stale coordinator repair copy no longer sends users to a removed tab.

The latest pass rewires Settings Local LLM stack install CTAs. Command-backed guided and advanced stack rows such as llama.cpp, LocalAI, and vLLM now show the same primary Install action as starter/easy rows, while still showing the reviewed command before visible Terminal handoff and keeping install detection separate from backend routing.

The previous pass simplifies HR Manager Manage subtab labels. Manage now shows Team ops, Overview, Relay, and Hierarchy; Manage keeps the existing lifecycle-only Team ops guardrails and routePane=operations wiring, while Structure start/stop and stale coordinator repair copy point operators to Manage > Team ops.

The previous pass makes Settings managed subscription sign-ins subscription-only. The Perplexity API setup row was removed from that card, the copy now points API-key providers to Inference backends, and the product spec/wiki document Perplexity as an API backend provider rather than a managed subscription runtime.

The previous pass simplifies Settings Local models. The card now shows a compact four-cell model/backend summary, primary Check catalog/Scan running/Stack setup actions, direct next-step setup copy, quiet local-concurrency control, passive installed-model chips, and catalog state in the summary row; duplicate scan buttons, synced/enabled provider counters, per-chip update links, the long Ollama explainer, and the LOCAL_MODEL_CONCURRENCY environment note are no longer rendered in the card.

The latest pass removes ambient shared-default port noise from Settings Local LLM stacks. Rows now warn only when a live scan or configured backend proves a current port conflict, while catalog-only overlaps such as llama.cpp, MLX, LocalAI, and TGI sharing common defaults stay quiet until the user actually configures or starts one.

The latest pass clarifies Computer Use macOS permission readback. Screen Recording and Accessibility remain strict capture/input gates, while Input Monitoring and Automation show amber manual-verification states when macOS blocks TCC inspection or has not recorded a per-app Automation target yet, so a Settings-listed IDACC app no longer looks falsely denied just because readback is unavailable.

The latest pass aligns Identity & Keys with Settings-backed agent chain access. Identity now reads the sanitized Agent chain RPC list from Settings, shows enabled networks as the operational chain allowlist for active granted keys, and keeps mock-vs-live signing mode explicit so configured RPCs do not imply real transaction broadcast until a live key provider is wired.

The latest pass makes synced API provider lanes assignable from Health. API/cloud lanes such as OpenRouter and NVIDIA now appear under API provider lanes once Settings has an enabled backend with a synced model list; choosing one stages the provider lane, switches the Model dropdown to that backend's models, and applies through the manager provider-api harness. IDACC resolves the provider key at write time and sends it process-local for the rebuild; the manager stores only non-secret lane metadata, while unsynced API lanes remain disabled until Connect & sync succeeds.

The latest pass simplifies Work Plans and removes stale promoted draft duplicates. Plans now keeps detailed status/tag grouping behind a Filters tray, exposes completed Brain plans through a Completed reveal or the Done filter, and deletes the source draft after promotion creates the live Pending Brain plan. Legacy promoted drafts tagged with `→ plan ...` are pruned from the draft list on reload, so a plan cannot sit in both Drafts and Plans after handoff.

The latest pass simplifies HR Manager Build Team maintenance. Rename/merge now lives in one compact row with mode, source, target/name, optional empty-source deletion, inline warnings, and a single action while preserving the same fresh-read, collision, default-team, coordinator/relay/secondary, Org Sync, and optional source-delete guardrails.

The latest pass fills the manager contract behind HR team maintenance. The id-agents manager now implements the advertised `/agents/:id/team` route, blocks non-admin moves, missing target teams, same-team moves, remote/public endpoints, and target name collisions, moves stopped agents without starting them, rebuilds only running local agents under the target team, broadcasts source/target roster changes, and adds focused integration coverage for the route.

The latest pass adds HR Manager validator roster optionality and renames the former Route tab to Manage. Manage now contains Team ops, Overview, and Hierarchy; default/coder and default/researcher remain protected validators, additional default-team validators can be added through a roster picker, bridge and org-sync normalization keep default/lead out of validator slots, and all add/remove/coverage writes recheck the effective secondary-lead stamp before org:setSecondaryLeads plus no-rebuild Org Sync.

The latest pass expands Brain fact-contradiction Inbox review cards. IDACC now extracts topic/field, competing fact IDs, readable claim summaries, source/confidence/timestamp evidence, source text units, repeated-cycle signals, and proposed apply requirements from the Brain approval payload; if the claims are missing, the card explicitly says not to approve from Inbox and to open Brain Health. The approve/reject labels now make contradiction review distinct from generic Brain changes, existing cards refresh through detailVersion 4, and Brain approval Inbox questions can store longer review text while ordinary blocker questions keep their compact limit.

## Boundaries

Dashboard stays observe, talk, and refresh, with talk targets resolved through the same hierarchy source HR Manager owns. Work owns plans, goals, tasks, loops, schedules, dreams, guarded Autopilot driver actions, and hierarchy-resolved cross-team work dispatch. Projects owns workspace tracking, repo links, reviewed git actions, and guarded direct commits. Health owns fleet observation, token-throughput evidence, probe/lifecycle actions, and runtime draft application, but it cannot delete the locked default leadership backbone or edit routing. Hidden context budgeting, historical replay, retrieval-plugin validation, and portable plugin adapter coverage live in backend dispatch guardrails, not Health controls. HR Manager owns hierarchy, relay policy, selected-agent instructions, team lifecycle, org sync, and first-run lead-routing presets; lifecycle controls live under Manage > Team ops, while Manage write panes recheck hierarchy, roster, relay policy, and org config after confirmation. Capabilities owns runtime-neutral MCP, skills, plugins, additive Brain catalog sync guarded by safe Brain core health plus current Skills contract, read-only Brain contract chips, safe Brain Core health, Brain Agents authority review, Brain Fleet count-drift and partial-authority review, exact team-scoped capability writes, implicit all-team/all-lead target-set rechecks, in-use skill delete lockouts, and guarded Brain dashboard handoffs. Identity owns local controller proof, wallet provisioning, scoped key actions, Settings-backed chain-allowlist visibility, legacy authority review, reviewed session-key presets, and read-only Brain /controllers comparison. Computer Use owns the Electron local broker, blessed-agent attachment, broker-acknowledged approval flow, immediate stop controls, unavailable-state disclosure in shells without the native broker, and amber manual-verification semantics for macOS permissions that cannot be directly read back. Settings owns local model/backend setup, local-first image generator routing with API fallback, and read-only manager-extension compatibility checks, but keeps coordinator/routing edits handed to HR Manager; local discovery rows are snapshots and stack configured/running badges are not package install authority. Brain Fleet, Health, Skills, Learning, Agents, and Graph remain read-only status surfaces; cached Brain Fleet rows, additive Brain skill graph nodes, and sanitized Graph entity data are diagnostic records, not live lifecycle, local-catalog, or credential authority, unless a future write flow receives its own guarded preview-and-apply path.

## Open decisions

Decide whether to add dedicated pure-read Brain Health/Learning summary endpoints, or keep the current read-only dashboard HTML over existing report routes while IDACC-owned workflows handle reviewed mutations. Decide separately whether IDACC should remain pure-client with guided manager instructions, open a reviewed Terminal manager updater, or bundle a compatible manager.
implementedOneHundredTwentyFifthPass: HR Manager Manage: removed the duplicate Instructions mode so Manage is lifecycle-only Team ops for start/stop/probe/rebuild/delete.

HR Manager Structure/Manage: added explicit Manage handoffs to Structure for selected-agent instruction edits and Route for hierarchy/org sync, keeping prompt writes and lifecycle actions on separate owner panes.
Validation: npm run typecheck passed in the published idctl-desktop after the HR Manager Manage simplification.
implementedOneHundredTwentySixthPass: Capabilities Skills: Preview & sync Brain is now disabled when Brain /skills/index is missing the current route/profile/facet/reuse/no-store contract, even if Brain core health is reachable.
Capabilities Skills: sync rechecks the latest Brain Skills contract immediately before the preview and blocks the additive Brain write when that contract is stale or unavailable.
Validation: npm run typecheck passed in the published idctl-desktop after the Brain Skills contract write block.
implementedOneHundredTwentySeventhPass: HR Manager Build: default/lead no longer gets stale bare-name route examples for legal/technology-security teams that are not part of the app-side first-run blueprint set.

HR Manager Build: default-primary route examples are team-qualified and aligned to the starter teams the app can prefill: engineering-team/engineering-lead, ops-team/ops-lead, research/research-lead, and onchain-execution/onchain-lead.
UX decision surfaced: optional legal and technology-security leads remain supported, but the default-primary preset now says they are valid only after those teams exist and are current in HR Manager.
Validation: npm run typecheck passed in the published idctl-desktop after the first-run routing preset tightening.
implementedOneHundredTwentyEighthPass: Dashboard: talk-to routing now reads org:hierarchy for all selected teams, not only the active team.
Dashboard: the embedded Chat lockTarget and selector label use the same hierarchy-resolved team lead, with the old lead/manager/first-agent heuristic only as a missing-coordinator fallback.

Risk removed: a non-active team can no longer show a team-lead label while dispatching to a stale bare lead guess when the actual coordinator is ops-team/ops-lead, engineering-team/engineering-lead, research/research-lead, onchain-execution/onchain-lead, or another HR-managed lead.
Validation: npm run typecheck passed in the published idctl-desktop after the Dashboard hierarchy-routing fix.
implementedOneHundredTwentyNinthPass: Work Tasks: Create Work now loads org:hierarchy and resolves the chosen work team through its current coordinator before using a lead target.
Work Tasks: team-leads assignment scope now selects hierarchy-resolved coordinators instead of using a lead/manager/first-agent heuristic for non-active teams.
Work Reconcile: triage now routes each pending team through the same hierarchy-resolved coordinator lookup before calling work:triage.
Validation: npm run typecheck passed in the published idctl-desktop after the Work hierarchy-routing fix.
implementedOneHundredThirtiethPass: Projects: Refine with lead and commit-message drafting now resolve the project team lead through org:hierarchy instead of using the currently active team lead.

The Fleet model-lanes panel labels linked unsupported subscriptions as adapter needed instead of current-only, so Settings account state is visible without letting it overwrite agent runtime status.
Validation: npm test in idctl, npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki drift, diff check, and npm run build passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredTwentyEighthPass: Health moved from the top-level sidebar into HR Manager as the tab order Structure, Health, Build, Manage.
The legacy health route and command-palette Health action now open HR Manager with the Health tab focused, preserving saved links while making HR Manager the visible owner page.
Health renders in an embedded mode inside HR Manager while keeping token throughput, Probe all, AgentTable, runtime/model staging, and model-lane guardrails intact.
Validation: npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki drift, diff check, and npm run build passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredTwentyNinthPass: HR Manager Build runtime pickers now share Health's Settings-led availability contract: assignable manager harnesses plus synced route-ready API provider lanes.

Team Builder model pickers now populate from the same provider-lane model catalog used by Health, so OpenRouter, NVIDIA, and similar API backends can be selected during one-click team builds when Settings has keyed and synced them.
Onboarding now resolves provider lanes in the main process after spawn, applies the selected provider model before rebuild, and keeps API keys process-local instead of storing them in renderer plans.
Validation: npm test in idctl and npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredThirtiethPass: HR Manager Build now runs automated runtime/model verification before the final build confirmation.
The verifier live-probes only the selected API provider lanes, refreshes their cached model lists, and blocks stale provider/model rows before onboarding starts.
Verified rows are summarized in the Build confirmation prompt and the inline Build status now shows runtime verification progress/results.
runtime:verifyAssignments invalidates Settings/module/Brain sync domains because selected provider model snapshots can refresh during verification.
implementedTwoHundredThirtyFirstPass: Settings now reconciles installed local provider stacks into disabled pending Inference backend rows even before the local API server is running.
Pending local rows show installed/start-server state and are upgraded in place when Scan running or Connect & sync proves a live model-listing endpoint.
Successful Connect & sync on a disabled loopback keyless provider now enables it automatically, so installed local stacks become route-ready once their server is live without a second manual toggle.
Validation: npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki JSON parse, and npm run build passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredThirtySecondPass: Local models & backends now performs a read-only Ollama library catalog check on Settings startup and every six hours while mounted.

The Ollama bridge fetches watched public tag pages, parses tag digest/size/context/update metadata, compares installed /api/tags digests, and surfaces explicit Update buttons only when a local digest differs from the remote library digest.
New public Ollama tags outside IDACC's curated local model catalog are surfaced as optional Download actions without automatically pulling models.
Validation: npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki JSON parse, direct catalog parser probe against Ollama Gemma4 tags, and npm run build passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredThirtyThirdPass: Local LLM stack rows no longer collapse unsupported or templated installs into the vague guided setup required label.
Unavailable install rows now render concrete short labels such as Linux host required, choose model first, or manual setup while retaining the full explanation in the tooltip.
Validation: npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki JSON parse, and npm run build passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredThirtyFourthPass: Local LLM stack port warnings now suppress self-conflicts when the live/configured port belongs to that same stack's configured backend or exact discovered server.
Stack claimed-port display no longer keeps parsing stale default install command ports after an installed stack has a detected/operator-selected alternate port.
Inference backend rows now render live no-model probes as warnings and no longer treat them as route-ready until a synced/probed model list exists.
Validation: npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki JSON parse, and npm run build passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredThirtyFifthPass: Settings Local image generator now distinguishes saved local preference from an unsaved URL draft; the placeholder no longer looks like a configured localhost URL, and Save is disabled until a real changed URL exists.
Scan local now checks configured loopback OpenAI-compatible provider URLs before common defaults, so alternate LocalAI-style image APIs such as a provider on port 8082 can be detected.
Configured provider detection handles base URLs that already end in /v1 without probing a duplicated /v1/v1/models path.
Validation: npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki JSON parse, and npm run build passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredThirtySixthPass: Settings boot crash fixed: stack command regex constants now live at module scope so early installed-stack reconciliation cannot reference PLACEHOLDER_CMD_RE before initialization.
The crash reproduced in the packaged v0.1.475 app as a blank renderer with Uncaught ReferenceError: Cannot access PLACEHOLDER_CMD_RE before initialization.
Validation: npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki JSON parse, npm run build, and local Electron start with renderer logging passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredThirtySeventhPass: Settings Local models now persists a sanitized app-side localModelCatalog overlay: public Ollama tags discovered by Check + add catalog are saved into the local searchable/downloadable catalog without rewriting the bundled reviewed model list.

The Ollama catalog checker now returns savedModels/savedCount, adds newly discovered tags idempotently, and the renderer de-dupes bundled reviewed entries from discovered overlay entries before rendering the browser.

Settings Local models & backends now surfaces non-Ollama local backend model sources from live discovery and synced local providers, so Jan, LocalAI, LM Studio, MLX, and similar OpenAI-compatible stacks show model counts/chips or an explicit start/sync state near the local stack controls. Inference backend rows for installed but unreachable LM Studio now explain that the app can be open while the local server is off and offer a direct Start server action before re-checking.
Validation: npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop and npm run build passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredThirtyEighthPass: Inference backend rows now explain local no-route states inline: live local backends with an empty model list say to load/download a model and re-check, while unreachable local backends say no API server is listening at the saved URL.
Local backend action buttons now rename Connect & sync to Re-check models or Re-check for live-empty and unreachable states, and expose a Stack setup handoff next to the affected local backend row.
Validation: npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop and npm run build passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredThirtyNinthPass: Settings LM Studio setup treats brew install --cask lm-studio as app install only, then exposes a Start server handoff that opens LM Studio and runs lms server start when the installed CLI is available; operators still need a loaded/downloaded model before Connect & sync can list models.
The local stack safe-command allowlist now permits reviewed macOS open commands, so GUI-first stacks can be launched from IDACC without collapsing install success into backend readiness.
Validation: npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki JSON parse, and npm run build passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredFortiethPass: GitHub Copilot is the first linked-subscription adapter pass: the id-agents manager now has a copilot runtime profile and Copilot CLI harness path, while IDACC's runtime catalog offers Copilot only when Settings has local CLI/account evidence.

The Copilot harness uses a private prompt-file handoff instead of placing the full agent task on the process command line, checks the copilot binary at manager preflight, and keeps model strings provider-owned.
Grok Build, Google Antigravity, Kiro, and legacy q remain adapter-needed/read-only linked subscription evidence until their manager harnesses are implemented.

Validation: npm run build:core and npx vitest run tests/unit/runtime-registry.test.ts in id-agents; npm test in idctl; npm run typecheck, wiki JSON parse, and npm run build in idctl-desktop passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredFortyFirstPass: Kiro is the second linked-subscription adapter pass: the id-agents manager now has a kiro-cli runtime profile, Kiro CLI harness, and `kiro-cli whoami` preflight before local agent spawn.
The Kiro harness runs `kiro-cli chat --no-interactive`, uses a private prompt-file handoff, supports Kiro model/effort selection, and passes resume ids through `--resume-id`.
IDACC now offers Kiro as an assignable harness only when Settings reports a signed-in Kiro CLI, and its dropdown uses the Kiro model catalog instead of a one-model placeholder.
Grok Build, Google Antigravity, and legacy q remain adapter-needed linked subscription evidence until their manager harnesses are implemented.

Validation: npm run build and npx vitest run tests/unit/runtime-registry.test.ts in id-agents; npm test in idctl; npm run typecheck, wiki JSON parse, and npm run build in idctl-desktop passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredFortySecondPass: Grok Build is the third linked-subscription adapter pass: the id-agents manager now has a grok runtime profile, Grok CLI harness, and `grok models` auth/model preflight before local agent spawn.

The Grok harness uses `grok --prompt-file` so full agent prompts stay out of argv, supports Grok model/effort selection, resume ids, and default bypass-permission execution consistent with local agent driving.

IDACC now offers Grok only when Settings confirms live Grok sign-in, merges `grok models` into the Health/HR model catalog, and labels the freshness source as Grok CLI models instead of curated fallback.
Google Antigravity and legacy q remain adapter-needed linked subscription evidence until their manager harnesses are implemented.

Validation: npm run build and npx vitest run tests/unit/runtime-registry.test.ts in id-agents; npm test in idctl; npm run typecheck, wiki JSON parse, and npm run build in idctl-desktop passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredFortyThirdPass: Google Antigravity is the fourth linked-subscription adapter pass: the id-agents manager now has an antigravity runtime profile, Antigravity CLI harness, and `agy models` auth/model preflight before local agent spawn.

The Antigravity harness runs `agy --print`, uses a private prompt-file handoff to keep full agent prompts off argv, supports Antigravity model selection and resume conversation ids, and applies the reviewed auto-approval flag by default.

IDACC now offers Antigravity only when Settings confirms live model access, merges `agy models` into the Health/HR model catalog, and labels freshness as Antigravity CLI models instead of adapter-needed linked evidence.
Legacy q remains adapter-needed linked subscription evidence until a manager harness is implemented or the row is retired.

Validation: npm run build and npx vitest run tests/unit/runtime-registry.test.ts in id-agents; npm test in idctl; npm run typecheck, wiki JSON parse, and npm run build in idctl-desktop passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredFortyFourthPass: Legacy Amazon Q (`q`) has been retired from linked runtime lane evidence: installed or logged-in q status no longer creates an adapter-needed Health/HR model lane.
Settings can still display the q row when the binary is installed, and existing q-assigned agents stay visible through the current-runtime keep path for review or migration.

The managed subscription adapter sweep now leaves Codex, Claude, Cursor, Copilot, Kiro, Grok, Antigravity, API provider lanes, and local lanes as the actionable picker set, with q treated as legacy compatibility only.
Validation: npm test in idctl; npm run typecheck, wiki JSON parse, and npm run build in idctl-desktop passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredFortyFifthPass: Settings now treats MLX package install, MLX background server process, and route-ready backend sync as separate states instead of implying that a saved backend is running.

MLX (mlx_lm.server) now defaults to port 8081, installs the Python package separately, and exposes Start background/Stop actions backed by main-process local-stack supervision with logs under the app data directory.
Installed-but-unreachable MLX backend rows now explain that no API server is listening, or that the background server is still downloading/loading before /v1/models answers.
Validation: npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki JSON parse, and npm run build passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredFortySixthPass: Settings Local LLM stacks no longer render shared-default port warnings for catalog-only overlaps such as llama.cpp, MLX, LocalAI, and TGI.
Install review and row warnings now react only to current-state port evidence: a live scanned port or an already configured backend on the claimed port.

implementedTwoHundredFortySeventhPass: Settings Local models no longer renders the separate "new catalog tags added" tile after a catalog check.
Newly discovered Ollama catalog tags still enter the searchable local catalog and the compact Catalog summary count, while failures remain visible as inline error text.

implementedTwoHundredFortyEighthPass: Settings Local models & backends now replaces the four status tiles and always-on Backend ready strip with one compact status line.
The card still shows next-step setup buttons only when routing is not ready, keeps catalog/update actions in place, and leaves installed model chips plus non-Ollama backend model rows intact.
Validation: npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki JSON parse, and npm run build passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredFortyNinthPass: Health/Fleet model lanes now hide the generic Ollama harness row when concrete synced local provider lanes exist, so operators see Local · Ollama and Local · LM Studio instead of duplicate Ollama/local entries.
Synced local provider lanes are now selectable in the Health/HR Harness dropdown through the manager provider-api lane contract, while API provider lanes remain grouped separately.

Google Antigravity re-check now tries both agy and antigravity CLI entry points, and the model-lane source label now says built-in defaults instead of curated fallback for runtimes without a live model-list API.
Validation: npm test in idctl; npm run typecheck and npm run build in idctl-desktop; npm run build:core in id-agents passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredFiftiethPass: Settings Local LLM stacks now removes the Jan, llama.cpp, MLX, LocalAI, vLLM, and Hugging Face TGI install cards from the visible shortlist for now.
The underlying discovery and backend support stays intact, so existing saved local backends or manually scanned live servers are not deleted by hiding those setup options.
Validation: npm run typecheck in idctl and idctl-desktop, npm test in idctl, wiki JSON parse, and npm run build in idctl-desktop passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredFiftyFirstPass: Settings Local models & backends now uses the former manual download strip as the Local concurrency selector, with a 1-16 dropdown and Set concurrency action backed by the manager local-concurrency route.
The duplicate Local concurrency row above the installed-model chips was removed, while starter, update, and catalog row downloads still call the existing model pull path.
Validation: npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki JSON parse, and npm run build in idctl-desktop passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredFiftySecondPass: Settings Local models & backends now frames the Ollama catalog search, capability filters, and downloadable model rows inside a dedicated bordered panel.
The catalog toolbar and scrollable model list gained clearer spacing, row boundaries, and hover treatment so the searchable model catalog reads as one coherent control group.
Validation: npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki JSON parse, and npm run build in idctl-desktop passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredFiftyThirdPass: Work Goal Autopilot now preserves default-team decompositions even when the lead returns team names or rough owner hints instead of exact team-lead agent names, mapping them to active team leads before creating tasks.

Work Learn now recovers stale processing rows: old processing materials with review artifacts become ready/blocked for review, while unfinished stale rows return to queued, and Process next runs this recovery before selecting work.
The Learn tab now surfaces stale processing materials with a Recover queue action and allows a stale selected material to be recovered instead of permanently disabling reprocessing.
Validation: npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop, wiki JSON parse, and npm run build in idctl-desktop passed before release validation.
implementedTwoHundredFiftyFourthPass: Dashboard Activity now detects task-shaped JSON replies from agent leads and presents them as draft work decompositions instead of raw COMMS rows.
Dashboard query activity also summarizes decomposition drafts when manager events carry a JSON array of proposed work items.
The Activity header now separates active team count, feed rows, tasks, and comms so a feed-size cap is not mistaken for active teams.
Validation: npm run typecheck in idctl-desktop and npm run build in idctl-desktop passed before release validation.

pages.body:
# Wiki

Wiki is both the human documentation page and the editable schematic for the Control Center UI. Each article explains the operator purpose of a page; the structured sections below preserve tabs, tiles, fields, controls, displays, routes, and source files for implementation detail.

## Use it for

- Search pages, controls, routes, source files, and notes from inside the desktop app.
- Confirm how a UI surface is intended to behave before editing code or filing follow-up work.
- Keep app navigation metadata and human documentation in one reviewed JSON artifact.

## Editing rule

The desktop app hot-reload polls CONTROL_CENTER_WIKI.json through the main-process wiki bridge. Sidebar labels, icons, order, and visibility still come from pages[].nav for implemented routes, so documentation edits must preserve that metadata and keep every page.body non-empty.
